question: The bottom athlete has a triangle locked. Look at the grips the bottom athlete is using underneath his legs. Why has he abandoned pulling the head to use this specific grip instead?
choices: Elevating the hips backward allows him to execute a sweep.
Pinning the lower body stops the top athlete from jumping over the guard.
Transitioning into a leg lock becomes possible from this specific angle.
Compressing the opponent prevents them from posturing while tightening the squeeze.
subject: submissions
